calendar.matters:
Petition To Romove R.G.Mendribil,Jr, As Successor Trustee For Breach Of Trust, To Compel Report And First And Final Accounting; Etc.
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nicole Aruda for John & Alisa Mendribil. Casey Cummins for (trustee) Richard Mendribil. Barbara Simon also present.

The Court asked why jurisdiction is not proper in Arizona. Ms. Aruda stated that this matter supersedes the Arizona matter by 2 months, that the terms of the trust refer to California law and that everyone is here. The Court asked for the place of administration. Ms. Aruda stated that it is Arizona but also here, that Richard commandeered the trusteeship, that he failed to tell Jack that he was nominated and so Jack did not assume authority as trustee, that there is a pattern of not disclosing information on the Arizona proceedings, that he has misled her clients for 6 months and that they have recently received accountings showing that he has spent $60,000.00 on attorney's fees. Mr. Cummins stated that Richard is the trustee and is responsible, that 3 of the 4 beneficiaries are here, that Richard is in Arizona, that Jack did not want to be trustee or executor of the estate, that a formal declination was filed in the estate but not in the trust, that the case started with a verbal discussion between John & Richard Mendribil, that John asked if it was true that he and his sister were cut out and was told that Richard believed it to be the case based on an amendment to the trust, that the amendment was not signed and no signed copy was found, that they attorney made it clear that it was an invalid document and that there was equal sharing of the estate, and that they do not understand why they are going through all of this litigation. Mr. Cummins further stated that in regards to jurisdiction there is no question that this court has some jurisdiction but so does Arizona, that the decedent lived in Arizona as does Richard, that the estate has all of the liquid assets, that the trust itself received a death benefit and 1/2 interest in the property, that the initial accounting was filed in the Arizona court but the action is on stay pending the California court ruling, that it makes sense to leave it in Arizona, that there is interplay between the estate and the trust and that the sole beneficiary of the estate is Richard as trustee of the trust. The Court asked what would happen if it agrees. Mr. Cummins stated that legally it will not affect the civil case but that it ought to, that there are allegations of elder abuse and 1 count of malpractice against the attorney who drafted the amendment. Ms. Aruda stated that in Richard's response he says that 3 weeks after the decedent's death he noticed that the amendment was not signed, that the conversation with Jack took place after he knew that the amendment was not valid, that Jack could not have refused to serve because he did not know he was nominated and has filed a declaration to the effect, that the fact that the civil case relies on the same facts is another reason it should stay here, that if it goes back to Arizona things will get worse, that they are not receiving information from Richard, that he denies that the $60,000.00 has been spent, that they need things to move forward and that they need a neutral third party for this to happen. The Court requested that the parties submit their respective proposed orders no later than March 3, 2016 and that the petition to remove R.G. Mendribil, Jr., as successor trustee for breach of trust, etc., will be under submission as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
